# KnowAlgo

> Free, deep-dive educational website for Data Structures & Algorithms. Every guide explains the intuition behind algorithms — not just how to implement them. Includes Python code, visual ASCII diagrams, complexity analysis, and full LeetCode solutions (90+ problems covered). Designed for software engineering interview preparation, covering all NeetCode 150 and Blind 75 patterns.

## Start Here

- [Interview Prep Guide](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/interview-prep.html): Master study guide. 8-week structured study plan, 90-problem curated list tagged with Blind 75 and NeetCode 150 coverage, 15-pattern recognition cheatsheet, 45-min interview strategy, and complexity rules of thumb.
- [Home / All Topics](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/index.html): Landing page with all 15 topic cards and links to every guide.

## Foundation Patterns (Start Here for Interviews)

- [Arrays & Strings](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/arrays-strings.html): Two pointers (opposite ends + fast/slow), sliding window (fixed size, variable size, "at most K" trick), prefix sums (1D + hash map + 2D), binary search (3 templates: exact match, left boundary, right boundary), rotated array search, binary search on answer. 8 LeetCode solutions: Best Time to Buy & Sell Stock, Container With Most Water, Product of Array Except Self, Minimum Size Subarray Sum, Longest Repeating Character Replacement, Find Min in Rotated Array, Search in Rotated Array, Trapping Rain Water.
- [Dynamic Programming](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/dp.html): Memoization vs tabulation, 1D DP (Fibonacci family, House Robber, coin change, word break, decode ways), 2D DP (grid paths, LCS, edit distance, 0/1 knapsack), LIS in O(n log n) using patience sort, space optimization with rolling arrays. 8 LeetCode solutions.
- [Backtracking](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/backtracking.html): Choose/Explore/Unchoose template, subsets with/without duplicates, permutations with/without duplicates + swap variant, combinations and combination sum (reuse vs no-reuse), grid backtracking with in-place sentinel marking, N-queens, Sudoku solver, pruning strategies. 8 LeetCode solutions.

## Array/Sequence Patterns

- [Intervals](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/intervals.html): Sort by start for merging, sort by end for activity selection/non-overlapping. Merge Intervals (linear scan), Insert Interval (3-phase scan: before, overlap, after), Meeting Rooms II (min-heap + sweep line), Non-Overlapping Intervals (greedy), Interval List Intersections (two pointers), Sweep Line framework. 7 LeetCode solutions.
- [Greedy Algorithms](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/greedy.html): Exchange argument proof for correctness, Greedy vs DP decision framework table. Jump Game I (greedy reach), Jump Game II (BFS levels), Gas Station (circular restart), Task Scheduler (cooling period), Candy (two-pass greedy), Partition Labels (last occurrence map), Two City Scheduling. 8 LeetCode solutions.
- [Bit Manipulation](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/bit-manipulation.html): XOR properties (a^a=0, a^0=a, commutative, associative), XOR to find unique elements, bit tricks toolkit (check/set/clear/toggle a bit, isolate lowest set bit, clear lowest set bit, is power of 2), Brian Kernighan's algorithm for counting set bits, parity with XOR folding, bitmask patterns (subset enumeration, bitmask DP for TSP), bitwise AND of range (common prefix). 8 LeetCode solutions.

## Tree Structures

- [Binary Tree](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/binary-tree.html): All 4 traversals iterative and recursive (pre/in/post/level-order), DFS patterns (return value up from subtrees, pass state down, LCA, global max), Morris traversal, serialize/deserialize. 6 LeetCode solutions.
- [Binary Search Tree](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/bst.html): BST insert/delete (3 cases: no child, one child, two children with in-order successor), BST search, kth smallest/largest, floor and ceiling, range queries, BSTIterator, sortedcontainers.SortedList. 6 LeetCode solutions.
- [Trie (Prefix Tree)](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/trie.html): Character-by-character storage, O(m) for all operations. Insert, search, startsWith, delete. Full implementation with TrieNode class, FrequencyTrie combining Trie + Heap for autocomplete ranking. 6 LeetCode solutions: Implement Trie, Design Add and Search Words, Word Search II, Replace Words, Search Suggestions System, Maximum XOR of Two Numbers.

## Graph Patterns

- [Graph: Traversal](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/graph.html): BFS and DFS on adjacency lists, grid BFS/DFS (4-directional and 8-directional), multi-source BFS, cycle detection in undirected graphs (DFS with visited set) and directed graphs (DFS with 3-color states), connected components, clone graph. 6 LeetCode solutions.
- [Graph: Weighted Algorithms](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/graph-algorithms.html): Dijkstra (min-heap, O((V+E) log V)), Bellman-Ford (negative weights, negative cycle detection), Floyd-Warshall (all-pairs shortest path), Topological Sort (Kahn's BFS + DFS), Union-Find DSU (path compression + rank), Kruskal's MST. 6 LeetCode solutions.

## Linear Data Structures

- [Heap / Priority Queue](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/heap.html): Min-heap and max-heap with Python's heapq (negate for max), heapify in O(n), Top-K patterns (fixed-size heap), two-heap pattern for median from stream, merge K sorted lists. 5 LeetCode solutions.
- [Stack & Queue](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/stack-queue.html): LIFO vs FIFO, monotonic stack for next greater/smaller element and histogram problems (Largest Rectangle), monotonic deque for sliding window maximum, queue using stacks. 6 LeetCode solutions.
- [Hash Map / Hash Table](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/hash-map.html): Hash function design, collision resolution (chaining vs open addressing), load factor, LRU Cache implementation with hash map + doubly linked list, group anagrams, top-K frequent elements. 6 LeetCode solutions.
- [Linked List](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/linked-list.html): Singly and doubly linked lists, Floyd's cycle detection algorithm, two-runner (fast/slow) technique, dummy head pattern, reverse in-place, reverse k-groups, merge sorted lists, find kth from end. 6 LeetCode solutions.

## Optional

- [llms-full.txt](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/llms-full.txt): Extended version with more detail per topic (if available).
- [sitemap.xml](https://anurag2809.github.io/know-algo/sitemap.xml): XML sitemap with all page URLs and last-modified dates.
